places.name: Haridwar
places.modernInfo: Where Ganga descends from the mountains to the plains. Har Ki Pauri ghat. One of 4 Kumbh Mela sites. Ganga Aarti at sunset.

scenes.title: Ganga descends to Earth
scenes.description: The Narada Purana describes how Narada convinced King Bhagirath to perform impossible penance to bring Ganga from heaven. After thousands of years, Ganga agreed to descend — but only if Shiva caught her in his matted hair first.

scenes.title: Nectar drops at Kumbh
scenes.description: When the gods and demons churned the ocean, a pot of nectar (kumbh) emerged. As Garuda flew with it, drops fell at 4 places — Haridwar, Prayagraj, Ujjain, Nashik. Every 12 years, Kumbh Mela commemorates this.

places.name: Naimisharanya
places.modernInfo: The forest where all 88,000 sages gathered to hear the Puranas from Suta Goswami. Still a major pilgrimage site.

scenes.title: The sages gather to hear the Puranas
scenes.description: 88,000 sages assembled at Naimisharanya to perform a yajna lasting thousands of years. Suta Goswami narrated all 18 Mahapuranas here — including the Narada Purana — making it the fountainhead of all Puranic knowledge.
